title: C++ backend and 100x speedup
summary:
I moved the Glassbox evolution loop from PyTorch to C++ using Eigen and pybind11. The PyTorch overhead was too slow. Removing nn.Module structs saved a lot of time. Now the search takes 2.5 seconds instead of 250.

I also replaced gradient descent with analytical SVD for the linear weights. This makes the math exact and very fast. I added OpenMP for multithreading too. The AST parsing now works directly in C++, so I don't need Python for that part. My basis regression can find x^2 with 0.0000 MSE in about 4 seconds now. 

Fixed some MSVC compiler errors with nested functions. Added parsimony pressure to stop overfitting. Code is much faster now.
commitMessages: feat(sr): optimize C++ evolution backend with SVD and OpenMP
feat(sr): Implement C++ Lamarckian refinement and AST string parser
feat(sr): Integrate C++ Eigen backend for 100x ONN evolution speedup
